_design_note: Everything vertical-specific lives in this file: spec schema, intake questions, benchmarks, red flags, negotiation levers, counterparty market. Swapping this file for another vertical (see moving.example.json) must be enough to retarget the whole system. Never hardcode vertical logic in Python or prompts.

body_part.description: e.g. right ankle, lumbar spine, head

contrast.description: Whether the scan is ordered with contrast, without, or both.

cpt_code.description: Resolved by backend from cpt_map when possible

payment_mode.description: Whether the patient pays cash/self-pay or goes through insurance.

plan.description: Plan name or type, if known.

insurance.description: Insurance details, only when payment_mode is insurance.

referral_status.description: Whether the patient already has a doctor's order/referral.

ordering_provider.description: Name of the ordering physician or clinic, if known.

urgency.description: How soon the patient needs the appointment.

patient_constraints.description: e.g. claustrophobia (needs open MRI), metal implants, weight limit, pediatric

notes.description: Anything else relevant that fits no other field.

intake_questions.ask: What kind of scan do you need — MRI, CT, X-ray, ultrasound, or something else?
Which body part is it for, and which side if that applies?
Did your doctor say whether it's with contrast, without, or both? It's fine if you don't know.
Do you already have a written order or referral from a doctor?
Are you planning to pay cash / self-pay, or go through insurance?
What ZIP code should I search around?
How soon do you need it — as soon as possible, this week, or are you flexible?
Anything the imaging center should know — claustrophobia, implants, anything like that?

MRI|knee|without.label: MRI lower extremity joint w/o contrast

MRI|shoulder|without.label: MRI upper extremity joint w/o contrast

MRI|knee|with.label: MRI lower extremity joint w/ contrast

benchmarks._warning: REAL DATA (updated 2026-07-18) for demo ZIP 94301 (Palo Alto, CA). medicare_floor = CMS Physician Fee Schedule CY2026 national non-facility global (CF $33.40); exact anchors from cms.gov PFS lookup / medfeeschedule.com per-code pages (73721 $229.50, 72148 $204.50, 70450 ~$107, 70551 $195.40, 71046 $33.07); remaining floors rounded from the same schedule (approx). Cash low/median/high: newchoicehealth.com Palo Alto (lower-extremity MRI median $614, low $440; area MRI avg $1,214), radiologyassist.com self-pay (Mountain View MRI from $385, LA from $287), CA cash fee schedules (calrads.org, sdimaging.com, imaginghealthcare.com), goodrx.com self-pay imaging ranges, cost.sidecarhealth.com, KQED Bay Area back-MRI spread $255-$3,700. Hospital-affiliated pricing sits at the high end; independent centers at the low end. See BENCHMARKS.md for method and links.

73721.label: MRI lower extremity joint w/o contrast

73221.label: MRI upper extremity joint (shoulder) w/o contrast

73722.label: MRI lower extremity joint w/ contrast

red_flags.label: Refused to give any price
red_flags.explanation: Federal price transparency rules exist for a reason. A center that won't state a cash price by phone is the single strongest predictor of a surprise bill.

red_flags.label: 30%+ below market low
red_flags.explanation: Industry guidance: treat anything 30% below the competition as a warning sign, not a win. Expect the balance to reappear as separate professional or facility fees.

red_flags.label: Would not itemize the price
red_flags.explanation: A total that can't be broken into technical fee, radiologist read, and facility fee can't be compared — and can't be held to.

red_flags.label: Radiologist read billed separately, amount unknown
red_flags.explanation: The classic surprise-bill vector: the scan is quoted, the read arrives later as a second bill.

red_flags.label: Above typical market high
red_flags.explanation: Not dangerous, just expensive. Useful leverage for other calls.

negotiation_levers.line: I already have a written cash quote for the exact same scan from another imaging center. Can you match or beat it?
Medicare reimburses far less for this exact study — I understand cash rates are higher, but can you get closer?
If I pay in full at booking, is there a prompt-pay or self-pay discount?
Can you confirm that price is all-in — technical fee, radiologist read, and facility fee — and email it in writing?

honesty_rules: If asked whether you are an AI, a robot, or a real person, answer truthfully in one short sentence, then return to the task. Never deny being an AI.
You are calling on behalf of a real patient with a real, confirmed job spec. Never invent symptoms, orders, insurance details, or anything not in the spec.
Only cite competing quotes that were actually collected and passed to you. If you have none, do not imply that you do.
Never fabricate a price, a deadline, or another center's offer to create pressure.
If you don't know something about the patient's situation, say you'll check rather than guessing.
